name: Lovie Austin
abstract: Cora "Lovie" Austin (September 19, 1887 – July 8, 1972) was an American Chicago bandleader, session musician, composer, singer, and arranger during the 1920s classic blues era. She and Lil Hardin Armstrong are often ranked as two of the best female jazz blues piano players of the period.

about_quotes.speaker: Alberta Hunter
about_quotes.evidence: when I wrote 'Down Hearted Blues'... I hummed this thing to her... she said, oh, maybe we can make a record of that.

about_quotes.speaker: Billy McBride and Mary McBride ("Mack and Mack")
about_quotes.evidence: INTERVIEWER: ...that was a Lovie Austin record, wasn't it? ... No, Lovie Austin played it, but that's our record.
about_quotes.doc_title: Billy McBride and Mary McBride ("Mack and Mack") Interview, 1959-07-01

about_quotes.speaker: William Russell
about_quotes.evidence: Oh, Lovie Austin tells in Chicago, too, that she had to notate some of Jelly's pieces.
